evidence: gh pr view 8 --repo tbrandenburg/sofathek - PR #8 CI failing on Level 1: Static Analysis
Identified failure: 'Install backend dependencies' step fails with exit code 1
Root issue: CI workflow runs 'cd backend && npm ci' but npm workspaces manage deps at root level
5-Why Analysis completed:
Why 1: npm ci in backend fails with 'no valid package-lock.json' error
Why 2: Workspace configuration conflicts with individual package-lock.json files
Why 3: Root package.json has recursive install script + individual workspaces have independent locks
Why 4: Root 'install' script calls 'npm install --workspaces' causing infinite loops
Why 5 (ROOT CAUSE): CI uses individual 'cd backend && npm ci' but project configured as npm workspaces + recursive script bug
SOLUTION: Fix CI to use workspace commands + fix recursive script in package.json
PLAN CREATED - Two main fixes needed:
1. CI Workflow Fix: Replace 'cd backend && npm ci' with root 'npm ci' + workspace commands
2. Recursive Script Fix: Remove recursive 'install' script from root package.json
3. Verify workspace setup: Ensure root package-lock.json exists for npm ci
Implementation approach: Fix CI workflow first, then package.json, then test
1. Fixed recursive script: Removed 'install' script from root package.json that caused infinite loops
2. Fixed CI workflow: Updated all jobs to use 'npm ci' instead of 'cd backend && npm ci'
3. Removed cache-dependency-path from CI as root package-lock.json is now used
4. Local testing: npm ci works, make type-check passes, make lint passes with 2 acceptable warnings
All fixes implemented and tested successfully
